title: Timeline spans across multiple boards
excerpt: I have built out an intricate process flow that spans through 3 boards. The timeline column is set to mirror throughout these boards to monitor our timeliness of completing a legal process that is set at 14 days by law. …

posts.author: ncolej (Nicole Johnson)
posts.cookedHtml: I have built out an intricate process flow that spans through 3 boards. The timeline column is set to mirror throughout these boards to monitor our timeliness of completing a legal process that is set at 14 days by law. I need this timeline to only be considered “Done on time” when the final status in the final board is marked as Complete. Currently, it is being mirrored as Complete from the status of a previous board which is only one stage of the process. Is it possible to have this timeline mirrored and only be considered Complete once the final status in the final board is changed to Complete?

posts.author: jennap (Jenna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @ncolej

To clarify, do you have Deadline Mode set on this Timeline Column but you need this to be connected to the status column of a board the timeline is being mirrored to and not the board it was originally created in?

If so, then this won’t work as you can’t set deadline mode up with a mirrored timeline. You will need to have the timeline created in this final board in order to have it’s status column be the one to register it as “Done” or not.

Check out this article for more details: What is Deadline Mode?

Does this cover what you were looking to do? If this is not what you are looking for, could you elaborate a bit more on what you are trying to achieve?

Otherwise, remember to mark as solved if this answers your question

Cheers,
Jenna
